summary.intent: Resume a previous development session on the 'ParsingEntireLog' branch of the gossamer project, which was building branch-log viewing functionality.
summary.outcome: Successfully reconstructed the session state; identified uncommitted changes across 4 files including a /api/branches endpoint, fetchBranches() API function, RepoBranchCell component, redacted thinking support, and database indexes.

repo: The project has a /api/branch-log endpoint and BranchLog screen that parse 'Entire-Checkpoint: <id>' trailers from git commit messages
The app uses a client/server architecture with renderer code in app/src/renderer/src/ and server code in src/server.ts
Prisma is used for the database layer with a LogEvent model
Sessions have a concept of 'redacted thinking' that needs UI representation in SessionDetail

code.finding: Added /api/branches endpoint that lists local git branches for a repo
Added fetchBranches() function to call the new /api/branches endpoint
Added RepoBranchCell component: a branch dropdown in the repos table that navigates to /branch-log route
Added composite indexes (sessionId, timestamp) and (sessionId, id) on LogEvent model for query performance

workflow: Memory files stored at ~/.claude/projects/ enable session resumption with full context reconstruction
Git diff is the primary mechanism for reconstructing uncommitted work state when resuming

friction: Session required manual reconstruction — no clean summary of prior stopping point was available
open_items: All 4 modified files have uncommitted changes that still need to be committed
Further work on the branch-log/branch viewing feature was planned but not started
RepoBranchCell branch dropdown navigation to /branch-log may need end-to-end testing
Database index additions in prisma/schema.prisma require a migration to be generated and applied
